slices.focus: Formal division of family caregiving duties
slices.summary: Caleb and Lena formally divided caregiving for his mother Dorothy. Caleb takes full ownership of Friday medical transport instead of ad-hoc splitting. Discussed strategies for managing Dorothy's resistance (coffee stops, one-page process sheet), protecting evening decompression time together, and building a post-task ritual. Relief mixed with concern about long-term sustainability.
open_loops: Dorothy may still resist the new arrangement despite careful framing
Whether Caleb will resent sole Friday responsibility long-term; needs periodic check-ins
Need to periodically assess whether arrangement actually reduces damage vs merely shifting pressure
decisions: Take sole ownership of mother's Friday medical transport, no longer splitting ad hoc with Lena
Create a one-page Friday process sheet recording appointment time, changes, next steps, and confirmation
Build a decompression ritual (sit in truck or breakfast stop) after Friday tasks before coming home
